title: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices
text: Pearson correlation matrix gives one scalar per pair variables. Two numbers discarded collapse: shape association — linear, monotone non-linear, U-shaped, irregular. direction — whether yy smooth function xx differs general whether xx smooth function yy, leverage noise directional. janusplot() renders recoveries visually every pair matrix layout, using proper mgcv::gam() fits (loess) EDF, F-tests, random effects available.

title: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices
text: -diagonal cell shows: raw scatter (light grey), fitted spline (blue line) 95% CI ribbon, EDF (effective degrees freedom) bottom-right, n used bottom-left, signif-glyph top-right (*** / ** / * / ·). cell fill keyed EDF: darker = non-linear.
code: library(janusplot)  # Four numeric columns from mtcars (32 rows: small but illustrative) janusplot(mtcars[, c("mpg", "hp", "wt", "qsec")])

title: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices
text: synthetic quadratic + sinusoidal example. matrix makes obvious variables genuinely non-linearly related .  EDF x2 ~ s(x1) x3 ~ s(x1) clearly exceed 1; cell fills reflect . Cells involving x4 close EDF = 1 (linear / flat).
code: n <- 300 x1 <- runif(n, -3, 3) x2 <- x1^2 + rnorm(n, sd = 0.6)   # quadratic on x1 x3 <- sin(x1) + rnorm(n, sd = 0.4) # sinusoidal on x1 x4 <- rnorm(n)                     # independent d  <- data.frame(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, x3 = x3, x4 = x4)  janusplot(d)

title: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices

code: # Re-create the heteroscedastic example n <- 400 het <- data.frame(   x = runif(n, 0, 5),   y = NA_real_ ) het$y <- 0.5 * het$x + rnorm(n, sd = 0.3 + 0.4 * het$x)  out <- janusplot_data(het, vars = c("x", "y")) out$pairs[[1L]]$edf_yx #> [1] 1.00021 out$pairs[[1L]]$edf_xy #> [1] 6.157621 out$pairs[[1L]]$asymmetry_index #> [1] 0.720527

title: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices
text: Every fitted smooth summarised two continuous indices two discrete counts. drive 24-category classifier appear columns janusplot(..., with_data = TRUE)$data fields entry janusplot_data()$pairs. Let f(x) fitted smooth dense grid 200 equally-spaced points across predictor range, f' f'' numerical first second derivatives. Let w(x) empirical density predictor grid, normalised sum(w) = 1. monotonicity_index (paper symbol M): M = sum(w * f') / sum(w * |f'|)   [-1, 1] +1 means strictly increasing, -1 strictly decreasing, 0 non-monotone curve (bowl, dome, wave). convexity_index (paper symbol C): C = sum(w * f'') / sum(w * |f''|)   [-1, 1] +1 means globally convex (bowl-), -1 globally concave (bowl-), 0 inflection-dominated (S-curve, sine, flat). indices density-weighted describe smooth data actually live, extrapolated tails, scale-invariant: replacing y * y + b leaves unchanged. n_turning_points — count interior extrema (sign changes f'), robust noise via lobe-mass weighting. n_inflections — count interior curvature flips (sign changes f''), robust counting. Together pair (n_turning_points, n_inflections) drives primary shape_category dispatch; (monotonicity_index, convexity_index) disambiguate within monotone (0, 0) single-extremum (1, 0) cells. full taxonomy 2-letter codes, archetypes, thumbnail curves available janusplot_shape_hierarchy() rendered standing legend every janusplot() call. Tune thresholds applied indices via janusplot_shape_cutoffs(). See shape-recognition-sensitivity vignette faithfully classifier recovers ground-truth shapes across sample-size noise regimes.

Zero crossings localise turning points f̂\hat f; sign f̂′\hat f' gives direction monotonicity; magnitude gives sensitivity operating point xx. control engineering literally process gain K(x)=∂y/∂uK(x) = \partial y / \partial u gain-scheduled controllers built around (Rugh & Shamma, 2000; Leith & Leithead, 2000). causal analysis continuous treatment derivative dose–response curve μ′(t)=∂𝔼[Y(t)]/∂t\mu'(t) = \partial \mathbb{E}[Y(t)] / \partial t, Zhang & Chen (2025) argue often treatment-effect object interest, curve . f̂″(x)\hat f''(x) — local curvature. what: Noise amplification and why we cap at k=2k = 2
title: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices
text: Finite differencing raw data amplifies noise; penalised splines eliminate amplification, trade bias via REML-selected smoothing parameter. mgcv’s default thin-plate penalty ∫(f″)2\int (f'')^2, directly regularises f̂′\hat f' bounds (penalise) f̂″\hat f'' via basis rank (Wood, 2017, §5.3; Eilers & Marx, 1996). practice find f̂(3)\hat f^{(3)} dominated noise n<104n < 10^4 moderate kk, janusplot refuses k≥3k \ge 3 design. domain-specific reason need higher-order derivative, specify matching-order P-spline penalty explicitly (Eilers, Marx & Durbán, 2015) extract janusplot_data().

title: Shape-recognition sensitivity study
text: combination ground-truth shape, sample size n, noise level sigma, sweep: Generates n points noiseless canonical curve x ∈ [0, 1], y normalised [0, 1] sigma fraction y-range Gaussian noise contributes — SNR-comparable scale across shapes. Fits mgcv::gam(y ~ s(x), method = "REML"). Classifies fit via janusplot_shape_metrics(). Records correctness fine (24-category) archetype (7-family) levels. design factors orthogonal replicated. See ?janusplot_shape_sensitivity function surface. 14 canonical ground-truth shapes cover five seven archetypes (chaotic degenerate realistic deterministic generator).
code: library(janusplot) library(ggplot2)  janusplot_shape_sensitivity_shapes() #>  [1] "linear_up"    "linear_down"  "convex_up"    "concave_up"   "convex_down"  #>  [6] "concave_down" "s_shape"      "u_shape"      "inverted_u"   "skewed_peak"  #> [11] "broad_peak"   "wave"         "bimodal"      "bi_wave"

title: Shape-recognition sensitivity study
text: sweep’s hypotheses pinned simulation/PLAN.md (Scenario 4): H1. n = 500, sigma = 0.05, archetype accuracy exceeds 0.90 every shape. H2. Fine-category accuracy exceeds 0.75 n = 500, sigma = 0.05 monotone + unimodal shapes; wave multimodal tolerate less noise. H3. Rippled variants require n ≥ 200 sigma ≤ 0.10 resolve. H4. sigma = 0.40, archetype accuracy collapses 0.50 simplest shapes.

title: Shape-recognition sensitivity study

title: janusplot — asymmetric smoothed-association matrices
text: Asymmetric, GAM-based smoothed-association matrices continuous variables. -diagonal cell shows directional mgcv::gam(y ~ s(x)) fit, upper lower triangles tell different stories whenever relationship genuinely asymmetric — precisely scalar Pearson correlation loses information. Two novelties, paired asymmetry story: Asymmetry index = |EDF_yx − EDF_xy| / (EDF_yx + EDF_xy) ∈ [0, 1] — single-number summary directional disparity per pair. 24-category shape taxonomy — fitted smooth classified one 24 named shapes (linear_up, skewed_peak, bimodal, bi_wave, rippled_monotone, …) via (T, ) dispatch turning-point inflection counts, monotonicity/convexity indices disambiguation. taxonomy rolls three broader tiers: archetype (7), monotonic (3), linear (2) — grounded shape-constrained regression (Pya & Wood 2015), dose-response pharmacology (Calabrese 2008), Morse critical- point classification (Milnor 1963).

title: janusplot: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices via GAM Fits — janusplot-package
text: janusplot renders pairwise, asymmetric smoothed-association matrices continuous variables. cell shows fitted spline mgcv::gam() model, upper lower triangles encoding two directional regressions y ~ s(x) x ~ s(y) respectively. Unlike Pearson correlation matrix (one scalar per pair, symmetric), smoothed-association matrix gives two curves per pair intentionally asymmetric. Heteroscedasticity, leverage, directional non-linearity become visually evident.

title: janusplot: Asymmetric Smoothed-Association Matrices via GAM Fits — janusplot-package
text: pair, asymmetry index A_ij = |EDF_yx - EDF_xy| / (EDF_yx + EDF_xy) bounded [0, 1]. Values near 0 indicate symmetric complexity; values near 1 indicate two directional fits differ sharply effective degrees freedom. additive noise model (Hoyer et al. 2009; Peters et al. 2014), two directional regressions generally asymmetric data-generating process non-linear, asymmetry identifies causal direction mild conditions. asymmetry index offered visual pre-discovery diagnostic rather causal inference procedure; see package vignette accompanying paper full scope limitations (particular failure modes heteroscedasticity, confounding, Gaussian-linear DGPs).

title: Asymmetric smoothed-association matrix — janusplot
text: data data frame numeric columns include. vars Character vector column names use. NULL (default) uses numeric columns data. Non-numeric columns trigger error listing offenders. adjust one-sided formula RHS giving additional covariates /random effects include every pairwise GAM. example, adjust = ~ s(age) + s(site, bs = "re") fits gam(y ~ s(x) + s(age) + s(site, bs = "re")) pair. Default NULL fits unadjusted pairwise smooths. method Smoothing-parameter estimation method passed mgcv::gam(). Default "REML" per mgcv recommendation. k Integer, named list mapping variable names integers. Basis dimension s(). Default -1L (mgcv's automatic choice). bs Basis type s(). Default "tp" (thin plate). order One "original" (default), "hclust" (reorder hierarchical clustering Pearson correlations), "alphabetical". show_data Logical. TRUE (default), overlay raw data points (low alpha) behind spline. applies display = "fit"; derivative panels never overlay raw data. show_ci Logical. TRUE (default), overlay 95% confidence envelope predict(gam, se.fit = TRUE) fit panel (.e. display = "fit"). CI rendering derivative panels controlled separately derivative_ci. display One "fit" (default), "d1", "d2". Selects single quantity rendered every -diagonal cell matrix. "fit" — fitted smooth \(\hat f(x)\); default, behaviour identical pre-derivative release. "d1" — first derivative \(\hat f'(x)\) fitted smooth. Zero crossings localise turning points \(\hat f\). "d2" — second derivative \(\hat f''(x)\). Zero crossings localise inflection points \(\hat f\). single matrix shows single quantity design: stacked multi-panel cells crowd matrix realistic variable count. compare fit derivative, render two three janusplot() calls side--side; call keeps with_data = TRUE summary table tagged display column. Orders \(k \ge 3\) exposed — higher-order derivatives penalised regression splines amplify noise rarely carry usable signal realistic sample sizes. See vignette("janusplot") theoretical justification applied use-cases. derivative_ci One "none" (default), "pointwise", "simultaneous". Controls whether — — 95% confidence ribbon drawn underneath derivative curve display %% c("d1", "d2"). Ignored display = "fit". "none" — ribbon. curve zero reference line see. Default, pointwise ribbons overshoot nominal coverage joint region can invite -reading local features. "pointwise" — 95% pointwise ribbon \(\sqrt{\mathrm{diag}(D V_p D^\top)}\) (Wood 2017 §7.2.4). Valid marginally; simultaneous statement. "simultaneous" — 95% simultaneous band via Monte Carlo construction Ruppert, Wand & Carroll (2003) popularised GAMs Simpson (2018, Frontiers Ecol. Evol. 6:149): draw \(B\) samples \(\tilde{\boldsymbol\beta} \sim     \mathcal{N}(\hat{\boldsymbol\beta}, V_p)\), compute \(\max_x |D_i(\tilde{\boldsymbol\beta} -     \hat{\boldsymbol\beta})| / \mathrm{se}_i\), use \((1-\alpha)\) quantile critical multiplier pointwise SE. Valid feature localisation ("\(\hat f'(x)\) significantly non-zero"). derivative_ci_nsim Integer. Number Monte Carlo samples used derivative_ci = "simultaneous". Default 1000L — compromise coverage accuracy (Simpson 2018 uses 10000) CPU budget across every pair medium-sized matrix. Ignored derivative_ci. n_grid Integer NULL. Number equally-spaced points used evaluate fitted smooth (derivatives). Default NULL resolves 100 display = "fit" 200 otherwise, finite-difference second derivatives visibly degrade \(\sim 150\) points moderate-k smooths. Supplying n_grid directly overrides defaults. Larger grids shift numerical shape-metric values (\(M\), \(C\), turning / inflection counts) slightly computed grid. Shapes asymmetry primary reading; M, C counts secondary diagnostics grid-induced drift tolerable. colour_by One "pearson" (default), "spearman", "kendall", "edf", "deviance_gap", "none". Encodes per-cell fill colour chosen scalar. Correlation choices use diverging palette limits c(-1, 1) shared corr colour-bar title; "edf" "deviance_gap" use sequential palette labelled metric. fill_by Deprecated alias colour_by. supplied emits single soft deprecation warning forwarded colour_by. palette Character. Colour palette cell fill scale. Defaults "RdBu" colour_by correlation "viridis" otherwise. Sequential choices: "viridis", "magma", "inferno", "plasma", "cividis", "mako", "rocket", "turbo" (CB-safe), "YlOrRd", "YlGnBu", "Blues", "Greens". Diverging choices: "RdYlBu", "RdBu", "PuOr", "Spectral" (CB-safe). Passing sequential palette colour_by correlation silently upgrades default diverging palette. annotations Character vector, subset c("edf", "", "shape", "code"). Controls corner annotations appear -diagonal cell: "code" — 2-letter ASCII shape code, top-left corner. "" "edf" — asymmetry index effective degrees freedom, stacked bottom-left. "shape" — shape glyph (Unicode ASCII per glyph_style), bottom-right corner. Default c("edf", ""). "code" "shape" occupy distinct corners can requested together. See janusplot_shape_hierarchy() full code list. shape_cutoffs Named list classification thresholds used map continuous shape indices discrete shape_category labels; see janusplot_shape_cutoffs(). show_shape_legend Logical. TRUE (default), attach standing shape-types legend plate matrix illustrates every category taxonomy canonical thumbnail spline. Independent annotations. glyph_style One "ascii" (default) "unicode". Controls cell shape glyphs render "shape" included annotations. Default "ascii" maximum portability across typesetting pipelines; switch "unicode" target font known cover curve glyph set. labels One "border" (default), "diagonal", "none". Controls variable names rendered: "border" — names along top (rotated per label_srt) left margins matrix; diagonal cells left blank. Mirrors corrplot's tl.pos = "lt" convention. "diagonal" — names centred diagonal cells (pre-0.1 layout). "none" — labels suppressed entirely; diagonal cells blank. diagonal One "auto" (default), "blank", "name", "density". Controls rendered diagonal cells matrix. "auto" — preserves historical behaviour: variable name labels = "diagonal", blank otherwise. "blank" — empty bordered panel (uniform grid reading). "name" — variable name centred cell, bold. "density" — kernel density variable filled translucent grey, rug raw values along bottom edge. Mirrors GGally::ggpairs convention; surfaces tail weight, bimodality, support clipping pairwise smooths alone reveal. Variable names come border (labels = "border", default) mode . label_srt Numeric. Rotation (degrees) top labels labels = "border". Default 45; set 0 horizontal 90 vertical. Ignored labels != "border". label_cex Positive numeric multiplier border-label font size. Default 1. Ignored labels = "none". signif_glyph Logical. TRUE (default), annotate cells · / * / ** reflecting smooth's F-test p-value. show_asymmetry Deprecated. Use annotations instead ("" %% annotations). supplied, soft deprecation warning fires argument merged annotations. na_action One "pairwise" (default; per-cell complete observations) "complete" (listwise; cells use rows). parallel Logical. TRUE, use future.apply::future_mapply() fit pairs parallel. Requires future.apply package user-configured future::plan(). Default FALSE. with_data Logical. TRUE, return two-element list list(plot, data) data flat per-cell summary (one row per -diagonal cell) everything plot displays. data element always plain data.frame (base R — data.table dependency). Default FALSE — case ggplot returned. text_scale_diag Positive numeric multiplier applied diagonal variable-name labels. Default 1. Diagonal labels additionally auto-shrink long variable names (nchar(var) > 10) fit cell regardless value. text_scale_off_diag Positive numeric multiplier applied -diagonal annotations (n / EDF readouts, significance glyphs, asymmetry-index labels). Default 1. Use < 1 cells small annotations crowd fit line; use > 1 presentation plots. show_glossary Logical. TRUE (default), attach multi-line caption matrix describing -plot abbreviations (n, EDF, , fill encoding, significance glyphs). keys actually displayed listed. glossary_scale Positive numeric multiplier glossary caption font size. Default 1. ... Additional arguments passed mgcv::gam().

title: Asymmetric smoothed-association matrix — janusplot
code: # Minimal runnable example — 3 variables, 6 asymmetric pairwise GAM fits. janusplot(mtcars[, c("mpg", "hp", "wt")])   # \donttest{ # Heteroscedastic DGP: Pearson r is ~ 0.9 but the inverse fit is # clearly non-linear, yielding asymmetry index > 0.5. set.seed(2026L) n  <- 200L x1 <- stats::runif(n, 0, 10) x2 <- x1 + stats::rnorm(n, sd = 0.2 * x1) janusplot(data.frame(x1 = x1, x2 = x2, x3 = stats::rnorm(n)))   # A single matrix renders a single quantity. To compare the fit # against its derivatives, render three calls and place them # side-by-side; each call's title makes the quantity explicit. set.seed(2026L) xs <- stats::runif(300L, -3, 3) df <- data.frame(   x  = xs,   y1 = sin(xs)  + stats::rnorm(300L, sd = 0.3),   y2 = xs^2     + stats::rnorm(300L, sd = 0.6) ) janusplot(df, display = "fit")  janusplot(df, display = "d1")  janusplot(df, display = "d2")   # Simultaneous CI bands on a derivative panel, per Simpson (2018). janusplot(df, display = "d1", derivative_ci = "simultaneous")  # }

what: Shape metrics for a fitted univariate smooth — janusplot_shape_metrics
title: Shape metrics for a fitted univariate smooth — janusplot_shape_metrics
text: Compute continuous monotonicity convexity indices, inflection turning-point counts, rule-based shape category fitted univariate smooth. Works either per-pair fit object returned janusplot internal machinery freshly fitted mgcv::gam() single s() term. indices bounded [-1, 1] weighted empirical density predictor: monotonicity_index (paper symbol M). Let f fitted smooth evaluated dense grid n_grid equally-spaced points across predictor range, f' numerical first derivative, w empirical density predictor grid sum(w) = 1. monotonicity_index = sum(w * f') / sum(w * |f'|) [-1, 1]. +1 strictly increasing, -1 strictly decreasing, 0 non-monotone. convexity_index (paper symbol C). f'' numerical second derivative grid, convexity_index = sum(w * f'') / sum(w * |f''|) [-1, 1]. +1 globally convex (bowl-), -1 globally concave (bowl-), 0 inflection-dominated (S-curve, sine, flat). indices scale-invariant (replacing y -> *y + b leaves unchanged) density-weighted describe smooth data actually live, extrapolated tails.

title: janusplot 0.1.0
text: Initial public release janusplot. package renders pairwise, asymmetric smoothed-association matrix continuous variables, cell showing fitted spline mgcv generalised additive model. Upper-triangle cells plot gam(x_j ~ s(x_i)); lower-triangle cells plot gam(x_i ~ s(x_j)). intentional asymmetry surfaces heteroscedasticity, leverage, directional non-linearity single scalar correlation hides. Public surface includes: janusplot() — matrix-plot entry point, per-cell fit / CI / raw-data controls, hclust-ordered variables, random-effects adjustment via s(g, bs = "re"), opt-parallel fits via future.apply. janusplot_data() — sibling returning fitted-model metadata (EDF, F-test p, asymmetry index, shape classification) without rendering. janusplot_shape_metrics() + janusplot_shape_cutoffs() + janusplot_shape_hierarchy() — 24-category shape taxonomy. janusplot_shape_sensitivity() + helpers — recovery-rate simulation 12 ground-truth shapes × 7 sample sizes × 500 replicates, precomputed shape_sensitivity_demo dataset quick inspection. remainder file documents pre-release development history, retained provenance.

what: Derivative views — single-display mode + Simpson 2018 MC CIs (2026-04-22)
title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: New display parameter janusplot(). Scalar — one "fit" (default, unchanged behaviour legacy callers), "d1", "d2". Controls single quantity rendered every -diagonal cell matrix. matrix-level title ("Direct fit", "First derivative f'(x)", "Second derivative f''(x)") names displayed quantity. compare fit vs derivative, issue two three janusplot() calls place side--side; call keeps with_data = TRUE summary table, now carries display column tagging rendered mode. LP-matrix derivative estimation. Derivatives computed analytically mgcv’s linear-predictor matrix via D %*% coef(fit) variance D %*% Vp %*% t(D). method Wood (2017, §7.2.4), popularised GAMs Simpson (2018, Frontiers Ecology Evolution). new dependency (gratia required). New derivative_ci parameter janusplot() janusplot_data(). One "none" (default), "pointwise", "simultaneous". Default "none" — CI ribbon drawn derivative panels unless caller opts , pointwise derivative ribbons -read local features. "pointwise" draws fit ± 1.96 * se LP-matrix SE. "simultaneous" draws Monte Carlo critical-multiplier bands per Simpson (2018): draw 𝛃̃b∼N(𝛃̂,Vp)\tilde{\boldsymbol\beta}_b \sim N(\hat{\boldsymbol\beta}, V_p) use (1−α)(1-\alpha) quantile normalised max-deviation statistic critical multiplier pointwise SE. New derivative_ci_nsim parameter. Integer number Monte Carlo samples used derivative_ci = "simultaneous". Default 1000L (Simpson 2018 uses 10000; 1000 throughput-quantile accuracy compromise affordable medium matrices). New n_grid parameter janusplot() janusplot_data(). Prediction-grid resolution. NULL (default) resolves 100 display = "fit" 200 otherwise. Callers may override. Larger grids shift shape-metric values (M, C, turning / inflection counts) slightly computed grid — deliberate side-effect, flagged ?janusplot Limitations section vignette. Shapes asymmetry primary matrix reading; M/C/counts secondary diagnostics. New derivatives parameter janusplot_data(). Integer vector orders 1:2 (multi-order allowed — unlike janusplot()’s scalar display, data companion returns arbitrary requested orders single call). Every per-pair list return carries deriv_yx deriv_xy named lists keyed order, data frame columns x, fit, se, lo, hi, ci_type. derivative_ci = "simultaneous" derivative frame also carries "crit_multiplier" attribute. New vignette section “Derivative views: theoretical justification applied use” — three worked examples (fit / d1 / d2) plus simultaneous-CI demo; covers LP-matrix variance propagation, noise amplification rationale order-2 hard cap, two applied framings (gain-scheduled control; derivative--dose-response causal estimand Zhang & Chen 2025). BibTeX section lives references/janusplot-derivatives.bib re-use R Journal paper. Validation. display enforces scalar choice via rlang::arg_match(); n_grid < 10 error n_grid > 500 raises informational note. Orders ≥ 3 refused design — higher-order derivatives penalised regression splines amplify noise beyond usable signal realistic sample sizes (Eilers, Marx & Durbán 2015). Breaking change interim development release. earlier -development pattern allowed display vector (display = c("fit", "d1")) stacked panels inside every cell. pattern removed; scalar API one supported. Callers reached stacked-panel intermediate must switch one janusplot() call per quantity.

title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: New labels parameter three modes: "border" (default — variable names along top + left margins, mirroring corrplot’s tl.pos = "lt" convention), "diagonal" (previous -matrix layout), "none" (suppressed). Default flipped "border" border labels free diagonal cells scale better k > 4 variables. New label_srt — rotation top labels labels = "border". Default 45° matches visual reference; 0 90 accepted. New label_cex — positive multiplier border-label font size. Default 1. Diagonal cells now rendered blank bordered panels whenever labels != "diagonal", giving matrix uniform grid reading.

title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: User-facing M C columns renamed monotonicity_index convexity_index across every data surface: flat data frame janusplot(..., with_data = TRUE), janusplot_data() per-pair lists (monotonicity_index_yx / convexity_index_yx / _xy variants), return janusplot_shape_metrics(), raw output janusplot_shape_sensitivity(), precomputed shape_sensitivity_demo dataset. Paper symbols M / C remain mnemonics documentation. Internal classifier parameter names (M, C) unchanged match math convention. New “Shape metrics explained” section janusplot vignette. Callers referencing result$M / result$C must update result$monotonicity_index / result$convexity_index.

what: Phase G — sensitivity study as a package feature (2026-04-21)
title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: janusplot_shape_sensitivity() — new public function runs full-factorial shape-recognition sensitivity sweep (shapes × sample sizes × noise levels × replicates) returns tidy raw data frame. Optional parallel dispatch via future.apply. janusplot_shape_sensitivity_shapes() — lists 14 canonical ground-truth shapes available sweep. janusplot_shape_sensitivity_summary() — per-cell accuracy aggregation fine (24-category) archetype (7-family) level. janusplot_shape_sensitivity_plot() — four built-diagnostic plots: fine / archetype confusion matrices, per-shape accuracy grid, recovery curves. shape_sensitivity_demo dataset — precomputed 2160-fit sweep shipped package vignette examples don’t need re-run sweep. Regenerated deterministically via data-raw/shape_sensitivity_demo.R. LazyData: true DESCRIPTION shape_sensitivity_demo accessible without explicit data() call default user expectations.

title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: Default correlation = Pearson (switched Spearman back first-render review). Spearman + Kendall remain colour_by options. Colour-bar title simplified corr three correlation encodings; actual method remains visible janusplot_data() column names caption. Taxonomy expanded 24 categories via (T, ) dispatch. New fine categories: skewed_peak, broad_peak, rippled_peak, wave, warped_wave, rippled_wave, complex_wave, bimodal, bimodal_ripple, bi_wave, bi_wave_ripple, rippled_monotone. Shape-types legend redesigned — now renders every category canonical 1-cm thumbnail spline 6-column grid matrix (font-independent, full-width). panel labelled label (code). old right-margin Unicode-glyph list retired. Cell glyphs default. annotations default c("edf", ""); opt per-cell shape markers annotations = c(..., "shape") (glyph) annotations = c(..., "code") (2-letter ASCII — safer font / PDF pipeline). passed, "code" wins. Hierarchy columns added every output. janusplot(..., with_data)$data gains shape_code, shape_archetype, shape_monotonic, shape_linear. janusplot_data() pairs gain per-direction shape_code_yx / shape_code_xy matching shape_archetype_*, shape_monotonic_*, shape_linear_*. New public function janusplot_shape_hierarchy() — returns 24-row taxonomy hierarchy columns (category, code, archetype, monotonic, linear, label, gloss). Intended downstream group-bys cross-referencing compact 2-letter codes appear cells data table. Classifier noise-robust. Sign-change detection now uses lobe-mass-weighted accounting, tail-noise wiggles smoothed saturating curve longer inflate inflection count complex. Academic framing. broader-tier vocabulary (linear / non-linear, monotone / non-monotone, convex / concave) standard calculus; archetype layer anchored Pya & Wood (2015) Stat & Comput (shape-constrained additive models) Calabrese (2008) Env Tox Chem (dose-response taxonomy). (T, ) dispatch coarsened Morse-theoretic critical-point classification (Milnor 1963).

what: Breaking / behaviour changes (2026-04-21 cell encoding redesign)
title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: Default cell colour now encodes Spearman rank correlation. per-cell fill previously encoded EDF (non-linearity). now encodes classical monotonic correlation via new colour_by argument diverging RdBu palette symmetric around zero. Choose colour_by = "pearson" / "kendall" correlation flavours colour_by = "edf" restore legacy encoding. fill_by deprecated — use colour_by. supplied, fill_by fires soft deprecation warning forwards colour_by one minor version. Corner annotations switched annotations — character vector (subset c("edf", "", "shape")) now controls corner labels render cell. "" (asymmetry index) replaces old n = ... annotation; "edf" keeps EDF visible text; "shape" draws new shape-category glyph bottom-right. show_asymmetry deprecated — use annotations. supplied, legacy argument fires soft deprecation warning merged annotations.

title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: Objective shape descriptor — every cell now carries two continuous indices (monotonicity M, convexity C), two discrete counts (n_turning_points, n_inflections), discrete shape_category 12-category taxonomy (linear_up, linear_down, convex_up, concave_up, convex_down, concave_down, u_shape, inverted_u, s_shape, complex, flat, indeterminate). Cutoffs tunable via janusplot_shape_cutoffs(). janusplot_shape_metrics() — public function compute shape descriptor fitted mgcv::gam single s() term. janusplot_shape_cutoffs() — public function returning default threshold list; callers override individual thresholds via .... Shape-types legend — annotations includes "shape", compact legend listing every category present matrix attached alongside colour bar. Toggle show_shape_legend. Unicode / ASCII glyph switch — new glyph_style argument ("unicode" default, "ascii" fallback) pipelines lack Unicode curve glyphs. Extended data table — janusplot(..., with_data = TRUE)$data gains columns cor_pearson, cor_spearman, cor_kendall, tie_ratio, M, C, n_turning_points, n_inflections, flat_range_ratio, shape_category, colour_value. legacy fill_value column renamed colour_value. Extended janusplot_data() pairs — per-pair element gains Pearson / Spearman / Kendall correlations, tie ratio, per-direction shape descriptors (M_yx, C_yx, M_xy, C_xy, n_turning_*, n_inflect_*, shape_yx, shape_xy).

title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: janusplot() — asymmetric smoothed-association matrix visualisation. pair numeric variables (X_i, X_j) != j, cell matrix position [, j] renders fitted spline mgcv::gam(X_j ~ s(X_i) + <adjust>). Upper lower triangles show two directional fits. Diagonal cells carry variable labels. janusplot_data() — programmatic companion returning raw GAM fits per-pair metrics (EDF, F-test p-value, deviance explained, asymmetry index) without constructing ggplot. Set keep_fits = TRUE retain full mgcv::gam objects. Asymmetry index — per-pair single-number summary |EDF_yx − EDF_xy| / (EDF_yx + EDF_xy), bounded [0, 1], exposed via janusplot_data()$pairs[[]]$asymmetry_index (optionally) cell corner annotation via show_asymmetry = TRUE. Partial smooths via adjust = — one-sided formula RHS (e.g. ~ s(z) + s(g, bs = "re")) propagates every pairwise GAM, producing covariate-adjusted smooths supporting random effects box. order = "hclust" — reorder variables hierarchical clustering 1 - |cor| (matching corrplot convention) group strongly correlated variables visually. na_action = "pairwise" (default) vs "complete" — per-pair complete observations annotated n_used per cell, vs listwise deletion. Parallelism via future.apply — set parallel = TRUE dispatch pair fits across user-configured future::plan(). viridis family (colourblind-safe sequential): viridis (default), magma, inferno, plasma, cividis, mako, rocket; viridis high-contrast: turbo (colourblind-safe); ColorBrewer diverging (colourblind-safe): RdYlBu, RdBu, PuOr; ColorBrewer diverging (colourblind-safe): Spectral; ColorBrewer sequential (colourblind-safe): YlOrRd, YlGnBu, Blues, Greens. Shared right-margin colourbar legend fill_by != "none", placed dedicated fixed-width column (1.8 cm) cells remain square. Cell annotations scale k — n EDF labels shrink sublinearly number variables grows, keeping small-cell plots legible. Glossary caption — dynamic caption matrix explains -plot abbreviations (n, EDF, , fill encoding, significance glyphs), showing keys actually displayed. with_data = TRUE — optional flat per-cell summary table returned alongside ggplot, data.table package installed data.frame otherwise.

title: janusplot 0.0.0.9001 (development)
text: 70 unit + integration tests; 5 vdiffr visual-regression snapshots. Passes R CMD check ---cran 0 ERRORs, 0 WARNINGs; 2 NOTEs remain (environmental: new-submission status; local HTML Tidy version). Three-scenario simulation study validating approach — linear recovery, non-linear detection, heteroscedastic asymmetry. Scenario 3 paper’s headline result: DGP Pearson r = 0.93, janusplot recovers asymmetry index ≈ 0.56 (IQR [0.52, 0.65]), exposing hidden directional structure scalar correlation misses.
